name: accela-mcp
description: MCP server for the Accela Construct API — search records, inspections, fees, documents, and more.
long_description:
Connects Claude Desktop to your Accela tenant. Search permits, look up parcels and contacts, list inspections, read fees and documents — all from chat.

## After installing

1. **Get your Accela app credentials.** On the [Accela Developer Portal](https://developer.accela.com), create an app and copy its App ID and App Secret. Use the redirect URI `http://localhost:8765/oauth/callback` (or pick your own and configure it on both sides).
2. **Fill in the Configuration tab below.** App ID, App Secret, Agency, Environment, and Redirect URI are required. Leave **Encryption Key** blank — the server generates one on first run and persists it locally.
3. **Pick which Accela features to expose.** Read tools are on by default; write tools are off and additionally require flipping **Allow Write Tools** as a master safety switch.
4. **Log in from chat.** Once installed, ask Claude:  *“Log me into Accela.”* Claude will call the `accela_login` tool, open your browser to Accela's authorize page, capture the redirect, and save tokens.
5. **Restart Claude Desktop** so the rest of the Accela tools become visible.

## In-chat tools that don't need login

- `accela_auth_status` — check whether you're logged in and when your token expires.
- `accela_login` — start (or repeat) the OAuth login.

If an Accela API call later returns 401, the response includes a hint pointing back at `accela_login`.

app_id.title: Accela App ID
app_id.description: Connection — From your app on the Accela Developer Portal (https://developer.accela.com).

app_secret.title: Accela App Secret
app_secret.description: Connection — From the same app on the Accela Developer Portal. Keep this private.

agency.title: Agency
agency.description: Connection — Your Accela agency name, e.g. NULLISLAND for the public sandbox or your jurisdiction's identifier for production.

environment.title: Environment
environment.description: Connection — Tenant environment, typically TEST, STAGE, or PROD. Match what's registered on the Developer Portal.

redirect_uri.title: OAuth Redirect URI
redirect_uri.description: Connection — Must match the redirect URI registered on your Accela app. Defaults to a loopback URL on port 8765.

mcp_key.title: Encryption Key (advanced)
mcp_key.description: Connection — Fernet key used to encrypt the local token file. Leave blank; the server generates one on first run and persists it under your platform's user config directory.

group_records_read.title: Records — Read
group_records_read.description: Read tools — Search records, get record details, read custom data.

group_documents_read.title: Documents — Read
group_documents_read.description: Read tools — List record documents and download content.

group_property_read.title: Property — Read
group_property_read.description: Read tools — Address, parcel, and owner lookups.

group_people_read.title: People — Read
group_people_read.description: Read tools — Contacts and licensed professionals.

group_workflow_read.title: Workflow — Read
group_workflow_read.description: Read tools — Workflow tasks and history for a record.

group_fees_read.title: Fees — Read
group_fees_read.description: Read tools — Fees, fee estimates, and invoices.

group_reference_data.title: Reference Data
group_reference_data.description: Read tools — Lookup tables: types, statuses, departments, fees.

writes_enabled.title: Allow Write Tools (master switch)
writes_enabled.description: Write tools — Master switch. Even with the individual write groups below toggled on, no write call mutates Accela data unless this is enabled.

group_records_write.title: Records — Write
group_records_write.description: Write tools — Create, finalize, and update records. Requires Allow Write Tools.

group_inspections_write.title: Inspections — Write
group_inspections_write.description: Write tools — Schedule, reschedule, cancel, result, and assign inspections. Requires Allow Write Tools.

group_documents_write.title: Documents — Write
group_documents_write.description: Write tools — Upload documents to a record. Requires Allow Write Tools.

group_workflow_write.title: Workflow — Write
group_workflow_write.description: Write tools — Advance and update workflow tasks. Requires Allow Write Tools.

group_payments_write.title: Payments — Write
group_payments_write.description: Write tools — Initiate and commit citizen payments. Requires Allow Write Tools AND Allow Real-Money Payments.

payments_real_money_allowed.title: Allow Real-Money Payments
payments_real_money_allowed.description: Write tools — Extra gate on top of Allow Write Tools for the Payments — Write group. Production environments require an additional flag in capabilities.yaml.

group_payments_read.title: Payments — Read
group_payments_read.description: Specialty tools — Read payments on a record. Off by default.

group_gis.title: GIS
group_gis.description: Specialty tools — Geocoding and reverse-geocoding helpers. Off by default.

group_reports.title: Reports
group_reports.description: Specialty tools — List and run agency-defined reports. Off by default.

group_admin_escape_hatch.title: Admin Escape Hatch
group_admin_escape_hatch.description: Specialty tools — Exposes accela_raw_request. Only useful when capabilities.yaml defines a path/method allowlist. Off by default.
